experiment: Bound A/D/E recalibration on valid-range cohort + label-noise floor
purpose: Refresh theoretical T3 ceilings (pre-hygiene D=0.683, A=0.351, E=0.171) under N=95 valid-range cohort and iter34 hygiene-corrected T1 predictions.

literature_floor.source: Goetz 2008 MDS-UPDRS Part III test-retest ICC

cohort.cohort_label: drop_allmissing_validrange (PD with ≥1 valid subitem + valid T1)

bound_D_pearson_r.definition: Pearson r(T1_actual, T3_actual) -- upper bound on CCC under perfect linear calibration of T1→T3.

bound_D_ccc_calibrated.definition: CCC(a + b·T1, T3) with (a,b) by OLS = 2r²/(1+r²).

bound_A_oracle_t1_meanR.definition: CCC(T1_actual + mean(R), T3) -- oracle T1 + mean-imputed R = IMU-only max if R has zero IMU signal.

bound_E_inductive_iter34.definition: CCC(iter34_T1_pred + mean(R), T3) -- realistic T3 ceiling under current T1 quality if R has zero IMU signal.

label_noise_corrected.note: Each bound divided by sqrt(ICC)=sqrt(0.81)≈0.9. Caps the deployable CCC against a noisy reference.
label_noise_corrected.deployment_caveat: These division-corrected numbers are upper bounds on observable CCC against a perfectly-rated gold standard; they BOUND ABOVE what we could observe even with an oracle predictor.

sota_contextualization.interpretation_short: If T3 canonical (0.3784) > Bound_E, the iter47 LOOCV result extracts more than constant-R inductive ceiling — meaning R (items 1-8, 15-18) has some IMU-recoverable signal at N=95 beyond the contribution of T1. If T3 canonical ≤ Bound_E, R is essentially constant-imputed in the canonical model.
